content: A black hole is a region in space where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape from it. This happens because a large amount of matter is packed into a very small area, creating an intense gravitational pull. Imagine squeezing the Earth until it is the size of a small marble—its gravity would become incredibly powerful in that tiny space. When something gets too close to a black hole, it passes a point called the “event horizon,” beyond which it cannot return. The event horizon acts like a one-way door—once crossed, nothing can escape the black hole’s pull. Scientists have observed the effects of black holes on nearby stars and light, confirming their existence and behavior. Studying black holes helps us learn about gravity, space, and the fundamental laws that govern our universe. This research drives advanced technology and innovation, some of which can lead to new tools and strategies in business.
